title: Sean Strickland slams 'f*cked up' Manziel boxing event
body:
The combat sports world was left reeling after the highly publicized boxing match between Johnny Manziel and Dylan Menery, with many prominent figures expressing their discontent with the event. Among them is UFC middleweight champion Sean Strickland, who has joined the chorus of critics condemning the bout as "f*cked up." Strickland's reaction is particularly noteworthy, given his involvement as a guest commentator for one of the fights on the card. The event, which was backed by a prominent figure in the MMA world, has sparked a heated debate about the intersection of sports and entertainment, with many questioning the motivations behind staging such a contest.

The Manziel vs. Menery fight was widely panned by fans and pundits alike, with many criticizing the lack of competitiveness and the perceived farcical nature of the event. Strickland's comments reflect the sentiment of many in the combat sports community, who feel that the bout was more of a spectacle than a genuine athletic competition. The UFC champion's willingness to speak out against the event is significant, as it highlights the growing concern among athletes and fans about the blurring of lines between sports and entertainment. As the combat sports world continues to evolve, it is likely that we will see more events like this, which will challenge the traditional notions of what constitutes a legitimate athletic competition.

Strickland's involvement in the event as a guest commentator adds a layer of complexity to his criticism. On one hand, his participation could be seen as hypocritical, given his vocal disdain for the event. On the other hand, it is possible that Strickland was drawn to the event out of curiosity or a desire to be involved in something new and different. Regardless of his motivations, Strickland's comments suggest that he has had a change of heart about the event, and is now willing to speak out against it. This shift in perspective is noteworthy, as it highlights the ongoing debate about the role of athletes in shaping the narrative around combat sports.

The backlash against the Manziel vs. Menery fight is not just about the event itself, but also about the broader cultural context in which it took place. The combat sports world is increasingly driven by a desire for spectacle and entertainment, with many promoters and athletes seeking to create events that will generate buzz and attract a wider audience. While this approach can be successful in terms of generating interest and revenue, it also raises important questions about the integrity of the sports themselves. As the combat sports world continues to evolve, it is likely that we will see more events like the Manziel vs. Menery fight, which will challenge our assumptions about what constitutes a legitimate athletic competition.

The reaction to the Manziel vs. Menery fight also highlights the ongoing tension between the combat sports world and the broader cultural landscape. The event was widely covered in the media, with many outlets treating it as a legitimate sports story. However, the backlash against the event suggests that many people are skeptical about the motivations behind staging such a contest. As the combat sports world continues to grow and evolve, it is likely that we will see more events like this, which will challenge our assumptions about the role of sports in society.

In conclusion, the controversy surrounding the Manziel vs. Menery fight is a symptom of a broader debate about the intersection of sports and entertainment. As the combat sports world continues to evolve, it is likely that we will see more events like this, which will challenge our assumptions about what constitutes a legitimate athletic competition. The reaction of athletes like Sean Strickland, who are willing to speak out against events that they feel are "f*cked up," suggests that there is a growing desire for authenticity and integrity in the combat sports world. As we look to the future, it will be interesting to see how this debate plays out, and what implications it will have for the combat sports world and beyond.
